name: A* Search
def: A best-first shortest-path search guided by a heuristic estimate of remaining cost.
problem: Find the cheapest path to a single target faster than uninformed search like Dijkstra.
mechanism: Expands the node minimising f = g (cost so far) + h (admissible heuristic estimate to goal); an admissible/consistent h guarantees optimality.
benefits: Far fewer expansions than Dijkstra for point-to-point
failures: Memory blow-up on huge maps (use IDA*/jump-point)
myths: "A* is always optimal" — only with an admissible (and ideally consistent) heuristic

complexity.Time: O(E) with good h; exp. worst
